Patch Set 1:

Please don't fix unlanded patches with other patches.  Sometimes that is OK if it is just cosmetic, but if the first patch is causing random crashes, it shouldn't land.

Patch Set 1:

hm, looks like this change is actually effective.

So what does it tells us? Should we merge this with the original kmap patch? land the original as is amd drop this one because our level of care for centos7 is reducing? something else?

Patch Set 1:

Oleg, I definitely don't think this patch should be dropped.  It should either be merged into the original, or landed in the same batch of patches (preferably sequentially).

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Oleg, I definitely don't think this patch should be dropped.  It should either be merged into the original, or landed in the same batch of patches (preferably sequentially).

It should be merged with the previous patch.

My intent was to verify this was indeed the fix needed for Centos7 before updating the initial patch.

commit.subject: LU-18925 libcfs: test prefer kmap() llite/dir.c
commit.message:
LU-18925 libcfs: test prefer kmap() llite/dir.c

Verify centos7

llite/dir.c changes might be at fault.

Move this to use kmap() instead of kmap_atomic()

HPE-bug-id: LUS-12822
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I437cb4b63786ee8f933e4cc16f4f29d7dcf05993
